rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical iodo-carbocyclization of dimethyl 2-allylmalonate with ethynylbenzene to give an iodo-substituted cyclopentene bearing a phenyl group, mediated by an electrogenerated iodine species in a divided cell.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (from NaI or NIS) to an electrophilic/radical iodine species that initiates radical addition to the alkyne; the cathode reduces protons/HOAc, not the iodomalonate intermediate (as shown by the divided-cell control with NIS giving no product).

key_intermediates.name: vinyl radical from iodine-radical addition to phenylacetylene
cyclopentene product bearing CH2I and Ph

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes iodide to I• (or I+), which adds to phenylacetylene to generate a vinyl radical; intramolecular/intermolecular radical cyclization with the allylmalonate forms a carbocyclic C-radical that abstracts/couples with iodine to give the iodo-cyclopentene; cathode reduces HOAc/protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Added as control to test radical intermediacy in the divided-cell reaction.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Added as control to test radical intermediacy.

probe_reagents.name: NIS (as putative iodomalonate precursor in divided cell)
probe_reagents.observation: In divided cell with NIS instead of NaI, no target product was detected, indicating reduction of an iodomalonate intermediate at the cathode is NOT on the productive pathway.

notes: Divided-cell experiment with NIS gave no product (n.d.), while the analogous divided-cell run with NaI gave 14% yield, supporting an anodic iodide-oxidation pathway rather than cathodic reduction of an iodomalonate. Page also documents the H-type divided cell apparatus (Figure S1).
